notes.content: The Gender Studies department emphasizes analytic and writing skills, qualitative research methods, and a historical and transnational understanding of social inequalities. The core courses address the concepts of gender and the body, gender and power, and gender and knowledge through a rang of topics such as freedom and liberty, social movements, masculinities, work and leisure, politics of social justice, intersectionality, colonial and sexual violence, and visual culture and citizenship. Listed below are the required lower division preparation course for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer: One course in Gender Studies. Only approved gender studies courses can satisfy the major requirement as indicated in the agreements by major. If your school does not offer an approved course, you still must complete one transferable course related to gender/women to be considered for this major. Courses in history, psychology, sociology, literature, anthropology, etc. related to women/gender are sufficient for admission purposes. All courses must be completed for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://gender.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: The community college course listed below has been approved to satisfy the preparation required for this major at UCLA, but it may not be the exact equivalent of the UCLA course listed.
notes.contentHtml:
The Gender Studies department emphasizes analytic and writing skills, qualitative research methods, and a historical and transnational understanding of social inequalities. The core courses address the concepts of gender and the body, gender and power, and gender and knowledge through a rang of topics such as freedom and liberty, social movements, masculinities, work and leisure, politics of social justice, intersectionality, colonial and sexual violence, and visual culture and citizenship.


Listed below are the required lower division preparation course for the major.


To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer:

  • One course in Gender Studies.

Only approved gender studies courses can satisfy the major requirement as indicated in the agreements by major. If your school does not offer an approved course, you still must complete one transferable course related to gender/women to be considered for this major. Courses in history, psychology, sociology, literature, anthropology, etc. related to women/gender are sufficient for admission purposes.

All courses must be completed for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://gender.ucla.edu and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: The community college course listed below has been approved to satisfy the preparation required for this major at UCLA, but it may not be the exact equivalent of the UCLA course listed.

notes.content: Students may also need to complete lower division departmental requisites after transfer to be eligible for upper division women studies courses in the disciplines. Disciplinary courses {e.g. Introductory anthropology/history/literature/psychology/sociology of gender/women} do NOT satisfy this requirement, but are recommended to demonstrate intention for this major.
notes.contentHtml: Students may also need to complete lower division departmental requisites after transfer to be eligible for upper division women studies courses in the disciplines. Disciplinary courses {e.g. Introductory anthropology/history/literature/psychology/sociology of gender/women} do NOT satisfy this requirement, but are recommended to demonstrate intention for this major.
